description_long: This object is a tripod with three black legs, each featuring visible joints and some colored tape or markings for identification or grip. The legs are splayed outward for stability, and one of the feet appears to be missing or detached. At the top, there is a stack of rectangular, layered wooden or composite plates, with a central hole likely for mounting equipment. The head mount is flat and simple, lacking any visible complex adjustment mechanisms. The tripod appears to be lightweight and portable, suitable for supporting small devices or cameras in an urban environment. The overall construction suggests a utilitarian design, possibly for temporary or makeshift use.
description: A black tripod with three legs and a stack of rectangular wooden plates as its head, featuring colored tape and a missing foot.
description_view_0: Front view showing the layered wooden head and three splayed legs, with colored tape visible on one leg.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the wooden head and the missing foot on one leg.
description_view_2: Rear view with a clear look at the rectangular head and the arrangement of the legs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the layered head and colored tape on the leg, with the missing foot more visible.
